audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for brotherly love, hospitality, prisoners, marriage, money, leaders, outside camp, sacrifice of praise, final blessing.

verses.restored: Let sibling-love remain.
Do not forget hospitality, for through this some unknowingly hosted messengers.
Remember prisoners as bound with them, and those mistreated, since you yourselves also are in body.
Let marriage be honored among all, and the bed undefiled; for sexually immoral/misusing and adulterers the Divine will judge.
Let your way be without love of silver/money, being content with present things; for he has said: I will never leave you nor forsake you.
So we say with boldness: Lord is my helper; I will not fear. What will a human do to me?
Remember your leaders, who spoke to you the word of the Divine; considering the outcome of their way, imitate the trust.
Jesus Messiah yesterday and today, the same also into the ages.
Do not be carried away by various and strange teachings; for it is beautiful/good for heart to be confirmed by grace, not foods, by which those walking were not benefited.
We have an altar from which those serving the tent have no authority to eat.
For bodies of those animals whose blood is brought into the holy places by the high priest concerning sin/misalignment are burned outside the camp.
Therefore Jesus also, so that he might sanctify the people through his own blood/life, suffered outside the gate.
So then let us go out to him outside the camp, bearing his reproach.
For here we do not have a remaining city, but we seek the one coming.
Through him, therefore, let us continually offer sacrifice of praise to the Divine, that is, fruit of lips confessing his name.
But do not forget doing good and sharing, for with such sacrifices the Divine is pleased.
Trust your leaders and yield, for they keep watch over your souls/lives as those who will give account, so that they may do this with joy and not groaning, for this would be unprofitable for you.
Pray concerning us, for we are persuaded that we have a beautiful/good conscience, desiring to conduct ourselves beautifully in all things.
And more abundantly I appeal to do this, so that I may be restored to you sooner.
Now the Divine of peace, the One leading up from dead ones the great Shepherd of the sheep, in blood/life of age-long covenant, our Lord Jesus,
may he equip you in every beautiful/good thing to do his will, doing in us what is pleasing before him through Jesus Messiah, to whom be glory into the ages of the ages. Amen.
But I appeal to you, siblings, bear with the word of encouragement, for indeed I wrote to you briefly.
Know that our sibling Timothy has been released, with whom, if he comes soon, I will see you.
Greet all your leaders and all the holy ones. Those from Italy greet you.
Grace be with all of you.

translation_notes: Hospitality and prisoner-solidarity are embodied ethics: remember them as bound with them, since you are in body.
Leadership yielding is accountable and mutual; leaders give account and may not dominate.
Outside the camp is solidarity with reproach and exclusion, not contempt for Israel's camp imagery.
safety_note: Hebrews 13:17 must not be used for authoritarian control. Leaders are accountable watchers, and the whole chapter frames ethics through sibling-love, hospitality, prisoner solidarity, doing good, sharing, and grace.
